prompt:
Our Saturday-market settlement script died tonight, right when the office wanted the numbers. Every run ends with ruby claiming the whole module is broken:

  >  9 | module StallBook
       | ^~~~~~ expected an `end` to close the `module` statement
  ...
  > 64 | end
       |    ^ unexpected end-of-input, assuming it is closing the parent top level context

  stall_book.rb:64: syntax errors found (SyntaxError)

Line 9 is the module line and line 64 is the last line of the file, so that narrows it down to... the entire file, thanks ruby. A teammate tidied up the block styles in this file yesterday afternoon (some do/end, some braces, he likes them consistent) and swears he only reformatted, and honestly every method I scroll past looks perfectly normal to me.

None of the settlement math is supposed to be different — commissions, table fees, the Monday chase list, all of it is pinned by test_stall_book.rb. Make `ruby test_stall_book.rb` run green with the smallest fix you can find. Don't rearrange the module, don't touch the test file.
